string.text: Out of food — the party ate a goblin ({GOBLIN}). Goblins left: {COUNT}.
Out of food — the orcs ate a prisoner ({PRISONER}). Prisoners left: {COUNT}.
You personally defeated in the brawl: {KILLS} of {TOTAL}.
(your allies finished off another {EXTRA}.)
You also got 1 orc and 1 goblin as a bonus for hunting different kinds.
You also got 1 orc as a bonus for hunting different kinds.
You also got 1 goblin as a bonus for hunting different kinds.
You earned {RENOWN_REWARD} renown and {TEEF_REWARD}{CR_ICON} Teef.
You successfully looted {TOTAL_GOLD} gold.
Recruit orcish elite
Da clan bosses brought out massive orc bruisers — they glare at you fierce, waitin' fer payment. You got {LH_PLAYER_GOLD}{GOLD_ICON}.
Orcish elite of the fortress
Already {LH_ELITE_COUNT} bruisers for {LH_REGULARS_COUNT} orc boyz. Limit — 1 bruiser per {LH_RATIO} boyz. Recruit more boyz first in villages and towns.
Not enough gold
Party is full
Recruit goblins (100{GOLD_ICON} for 2)
Need at least 100 gold
Goblins serve orcs — without orcs in your party they won't follow
You have {LH_GOBLIN_COUNT} goblins for {LH_ORC_COUNT} orcs. Limit — 4 goblins per orc. Recruit more orcs first.
Recruited: {TROOP}
Recruited: {TROOP} (+{COUNT} goblin servants)
Recruited: {COUNT} goblins (for {PRICE}{GOLD_ICON})
+{COUNT} goblin servants for {TROOP}
+{COUNT} goblins (wanted {DESIRED}, hit 4:1 limit)
{LH_TROOP_NAME} — {LH_TROOP_PRICE}{GOLD_ICON}
Arrer Boy Boss
Big'Un Orc
Black Orc
Boar Boy Big'Un
Orc Boss
Black Orc Brute
Black Orc Boss
Black Orc Berzerka
Orc Boy
Orc Choppa
Orc Arrer Boy
Orc Boar Boy
Not enough teef — recruited {COUNT} fewer orcs (spent {SPENT}, had {HAVE}).
Challenge an orc elite to a duel
Brutes line up in da lord's hall. Pick one — duel to da death, 1 on 1.
Duel with orcish elite
Only an orc can challenge an orc.
Not while da fort is besieged.
Dis fighter is still recovering. Wait {DAYS} more days.
This unit isn't loaded by TOR.
Victory! {TROOP} is slain. Reward: +{TEEF} teef{REWARD_TROOP}.
Defeat. {TROOP} proved tougher. Your wager is forfeit and you walk away in shame.
Take your reward
Walk away in shame
Challenge to a duel:
[Challenge] Unit '{TROOP_ID}' is not loaded.
[Challenge] No suitable scene in this castle (lordshall missing).
[Challenge] Not enough teef for the wager.
[Challenge] Not enough gold for the wager.
[Challenge] Error: {ERROR}
, and one of his kind joins your party
Lore-Hardcore: economy choice
This choice is locked into your save. To change it, start a new game.
Orcish Teef Economy (Warhammer canon)

Buy orcish elite in castles for teef (orc currency) earned in battle. Goblins still use gold. +5 teef per day for each orc town/castle you own. Lore-accurate to Warhammer Fantasy — orcs don't respect gold.
Classic (everything for gold)
Orcs and goblins are hired for gold as in vanilla TOR. No teef economy, no daily fief income. Simple and familiar.
Not enough teef: need {LH_TEEF_NEEDED}, have {LH_TEEF_HAVE}.
Only for orc heroes. Orcs don't respect outsiders.
+{TEEF} teef from your orc towns and castles ({COUNT})
+{TEEF} teef knocked out of {COUNT} seasoned enemies (T4+).
+{TEEF} teef for killing lord {NAME}.
Need {FEE} teef to issue the challenge (have {HAVE}).
Need {FEE} gold to issue the challenge (have {HAVE}).
-{TEEF} teef for {TROOP} ×{COUNT}.
Not enough teef for {TROOP} (need {NEED}, have {HAVE}).
Mod conflict: BetterTime + Lore-Hardcore

The third-party BetterTime mod is loaded together with Lore-Hardcore. This mod is NOT compatible with The Old Realms — it conflicts via UI MapBar override.{newline}{newline}Lore-Hardcore ALREADY INCLUDES BetterTime functionality:{newline}D3 / Numpad3 — fast forward ×4{newline}D4 / Numpad4 — ultra fast ×8{newline}Release the key — back to normal speed{newline}{newline}RECOMMENDATION: close the game, disable or remove third-party BetterTime in Bannerlord Launcher, restart.
Understood, I'll fix it
Da fight ain't over. No runnin'.
Enemy executed. The orc party takes pride. Morale +10, Honor reputation restored.
!!! THE FORCES OF CHAOS HAVE RISEN — a Chaos uprising has appeared on the map !!!
Lore-Hardcore — In-Game Guide
Pick a topic to read:
Back to topics
Tooth Economy

For orc and goblin players the Tooth Economy is ALWAYS active — no mid-game switch, no dialog at start. Humans, dwarves, vampires keep gold as before.{newline}{newline}When you play an orc:{newline}- In orc settlements you pay teef instead of gold for everything.{newline}- Outside orc settlements (Empire, Bretonnia, etc.) you still pay gold.{newline}- Teef are earned ONLY in real combat: kills of T4+ troops, lord kills, brawl wins, castle challenges, daily tribute from orc fiefs you own.{newline}- Orc parties drain no gold from your clan — wage morale penalty is suppressed (you don't pay them anyway).{newline}- Daily teef income is capped to keep AI orc lords from snowballing the map.
Career: from Bully to Boss

Orc career has two paths chosen during character creation: Path of the Bully (becomes Boss) or Path of the Shaman.{newline}{newline}Unlock requirements cut from vanilla TOR for Lore-Hardcore:{newline}- Tier 2 (Boss): weapon skills 80/80/30, 30 battles, 5 tournaments, 10 brawls, 2000 teef.{newline}- Tier 3 (Boss): weapon skills 150/120/60, 70 battles, 15 tournaments, 20 brawls, 5 lord duels, 5 cities captured, 10000 teef.{newline}- Tier 2 (Shaman): pray at a shrine, Spellcraft 50, Faith 50, 3 enchantments, 2000 teef, 5 shrines looted.{newline}- Tier 3 (Shaman): Spellcraft 120, Faith 100, 8 enchantments, 10000 teef, 5 cities, 30 shrines.
Orc Morale and WAAAGH!

Orc party morale and AI scaling have been rebalanced.{newline}{newline}- Orc lords have +40% HP and +30% to combat skills baseline.{newline}- Morale multiplier: x1.5 (orcs more aggressive than humans).{newline}- Elite orcs (Black Orc, Boss, Berzerka, Bigun) are FEARLESS — never panic from low morale or being outnumbered.{newline}- WAAAGH! proximity buff stacks when massed orcs attack.{newline}{newline}AI balancing (so orcs don't snowball the map):{newline}- Soft cap on AI orc lord party size: 80 troops.{newline}- Marshal's Army can hold maximum 5 parties (leader + 4 called) — orcs attack in mini-armies of 400-500 instead of 3000.{newline}- AI strength estimation: orcs counted at x1.8, goblins x0.8, trolls/Big'Uns/warbosses x3.0 — imperial AI now correctly assesses orc threat.{newline}- Massif Choppas reduced from 22 to 14 lords; Crooked Eye and Red Eye to 17. Capital prosperity rebalanced.{newline}{newline}Player party uses normal vanilla limits.
Castle Duel Challenge

When you visit an orc castle, you can challenge an elite orc to a duel for a teef stake.{newline}{newline}- Win: the elite joins your party as a trophy unit, plus you get +15 teef.{newline}- Lose: your stake is gone, your reputation in the castle drops temporarily.{newline}{newline}Elites available depend on the castle: Bigun, Boss, Black Orc, Brute, Berzerka. Each has its own price and difficulty.{newline}{newline}Stakes scale with elite tier — Bigun ~50 teef, Black Orc ~150 teef, Boss ~250 teef.{newline}{newline}The duel itself is a real 1v1 fight on a castle scene. No tricks, no auto-resolve.
Orc Sieges and the Ram

Orc sieges work differently in Lore-Hardcore.{newline}{newline}- Orcs CANNOT build trebuchets. Lore-wise: a counterweight machine with parabolic trajectory math is too engineered for an orc. Available siege engines: ballistas, onagers, catapults (including fire variants).{newline}- Battering ram is buffed: hits 5x harder, holds out 3.5x longer. Gates fall in 5-10 hits instead of vanilla 30+. Lore-wise: a log with a tooth-grinder skull, iron-clad, shoved by eight burly orcs.{newline}- Auto-resolve gets the same ram buff so orc sieges feel right whether you fight on the scene or skip.
Prisoner Cannibalism

Orcs do NOT chop prisoners on the spot. Meat comes ONLY from on-the-march hunger cannibalism.{newline}{newline}After a battle:{newline}- Auto-chop in loot screen: 0 meat (regardless of how many prisoners taken).{newline}- 'Chop' button in Quartermaster: 0 meat.{newline}- Meat from kills (TOR auto-formula): 0 for player victories.{newline}{newline}Only way to get meat from prisoners — take them, run out of food, wait. Each in-game hour with empty larder:{newline}1. PRISONERS first. Each = 1 meat.{newline}2. Then GOBLINS. Each = 1 meat.{newline}3. Lords and companions NEVER eaten (lord = ransom, companion = quest line).{newline}{newline}Lore: orcs eat captives alive on the road, not at the butcher's block.{newline}{newline}AI orc parties keep TOR vanilla meat-from-battle so they don't starve.
Greenskin Brawl Arena

Greenskin Brawl is a fistfight arena unique to orc settlements.{newline}{newline}- 18 enemies per match (much larger than the vanilla arena).{newline}- Reward calculated by your personal kill count, not just survival.{newline}- Runaway enemies who fall into out-of-bounds zones are auto-killed once per second to prevent the match from freezing.{newline}- Goblins no longer spawn on the arena roof (an old TOR placement bug).{newline}- Win condition: be the last one standing OR the team with most kills if you survive.{newline}- In conquered non-orc cities (Bretonnian, Imperial taverns) brawl atmosphere shaders are suppressed to avoid 'cannot find entry point' errors on older GPUs.{newline}{newline}Good for: training One-Handed skill, earning teef, completing the orc career quest brawl objective.
Executing Racial Enemies

When an orc player executes a captured racial enemy lord, the orc party celebrates — not laments.{newline}{newline}Vanilla Bannerlord penalises any execution with -1000 Honor XP. For an orc, killing a Dwarf, Vampire, Elf or Imperial lord is a feat, not a sin.{newline}{newline}Lore-Hardcore adds a buff:{newline}- Honor XP fully compensated (+1000) so the vanilla penalty is cancelled.{newline}- Mercy trait gets a token +1 XP (status balance).{newline}- Orc party gains +10 morale immediately.{newline}- A green message confirms the buff was applied.{newline}{newline}Applies only when the player is orc-culture (aserai in TOR) and the victim is a racial enemy: Dwarf (sturgia), Vampire Counts (khuzait), Empire, Bretonnia (vlandia), Wood Elves (battania), Eonir, Mousillon, Blood Dragons, Chaos, High Elves, Dark Elves.{newline}{newline}Executing your OWN race (other orcs) keeps the vanilla penalty — that's a shame for an orc warlord.
Lore-Hardcore Settings
Lore-Hardcore — Settings
Main menu — all parameters
Campaign — live only
Reset race
Reset all parameters of the selected race to defaults?
Reset ALL
Reset ALL parameters across all races to defaults? This affects everything.
Reset all
Coming in phase 0.2
Coming in a future phase
AI hiring
Gift of Chaos
Reset race
Reset ALL
applies immediately
applies within a second
applies to new spawns
Bannerlord needs restart
Orc HP (with player)
Multiplies orc HP in live battles you take part in. 1.0 = vanilla. Affects both allied and enemy orcs on the field. Example: 1.26 turns a 100 HP orc into 126 HP, so orcs survive a couple more hits.
Orc HP (AI vs AI)
HP multiplier for orcs in fights resolved between AIs without you (auto-resolve, off-map battles). Kept lower than the with-player value so AI orc armies are not unstoppable. Example: 1.08 = +8% HP.
Ogre HP (AI vs AI)

Separate, lower HP multiplier for huge units (trolls, Black Orcs, Big Uns) in AI-vs-AI battles. Off-screen the engine cannot surround them, so without this nerf 3 trolls can wipe 50 men. Example: 0.70 = -30% HP.
Goblin HP
HP multiplier for all goblins, meant to stay fragile fodder. Example: 0.40 = 40% of vanilla HP, so goblins die fast to offset their cheap mass recruitment.
Ally orc damage dealt
Damage multiplier for orcs fighting on YOUR side. Higher = your orcs hit harder. Example: 1.20 = +20% damage dealt by allied orcs.
Enemy orc damage dealt
Damage multiplier for orcs fighting AGAINST you. Lower = enemy orcs hit softer so battles stay winnable. Example: 0.70 = enemy orcs deal 70% damage.
Damage taken by ally orc
Damage TAKEN by orcs on your side. Lower = your orcs are tougher. Example: 0.92 = allied orcs take 8% less damage.
Damage taken by enemy orc
Damage TAKEN by enemy orcs. Higher = enemy orcs are easier to kill. Example: 1.15 = enemy orcs take 15% more damage from your troops.
Axe vs wooden shield
Bonus damage axes deal to wooden shields, letting orc axe troops smash shields fast. Example: 4.0 = 4x damage to wooden shields, breaking them in a couple of hits.
Mace/hammer vs metal shield

Bonus damage blunt weapons (maces, hammers, two-handed hammers) deal to METAL shields. Swords and axes barely scratch metal, but a crushing blow caves it in. Example: 4.0 = 4x damage, metal shields break in a couple of hits. 1.0 = vanilla (off).
Ogre damage to gates
Damage ogre-sized units (ogres, Big Uns, trolls) deal to gates and destructibles in live battle. Lower stops sieges ending instantly. Example: 0.20 = 20% of base structure damage.
Orc kick stun duration
How long an orc kick stuns the target. Higher = longer stagger so the orc can follow up. Example: 2.5 = stun lasts 2.5x vanilla.
Orc shield bash stun
Stun duration of an orc shield bash. Higher = enemies are staggered longer. Example: 1.8 = 1.8x vanilla stun.
Orc combat skills
Multiplier for orc weapon skills (one-handed, two-handed, polearm), which drive accuracy, damage and swing speed. Example: 1.30 = +30% skill, so orcs hit harder and more reliably.
Troll athletics
Move speed (athletics) of trolls. Lower = slower trolls that are easier to kite. Example: 0.85 = trolls move at 85% speed.
Goblin athletics
Athletics of goblins. Higher = faster, nimbler goblins that swarm and chase. Example: 1.60 = +60% athletics.
Goblin speed
Foot movement-speed multiplier for goblins. Higher = quicker goblins. Example: 1.50 = +50% speed.
WAAAGH! morale bonus
Morale the orc side gains per kill (the WAAAGH! effect). Higher = orcs rout less and snowball a winning fight. Example: 1.50 = +50% morale gain on kills.
AI lord party size cap
Maximum party size for an AI orc lord. Growth is capped instantly; oversized parties shrink over time. Example: 140 caps AI orc lords at 140 troops instead of growing endlessly.
Parties per WAAAGH!
How many parties can join one orc WAAAGH! (army). Army ceiling = (1 + this) x per-lord cap. Example: 4 = a marshal plus 4 lords.
Elite power (AI estimate)
How strong the AI THINKS elite orcs are when deciding to attack or flee. Must match the elite combat value or orcs misjudge fights. Higher = more aggressive. Example: 2.80.
Orc power (AI estimate)
The AI's estimate of regular orc strength for fight-or-flee decisions. Keep equal to the orc combat value. Example: 1.60.
Goblin power (AI estimate)
The AI's estimate of goblin strength for fight-or-flee decisions. Keep equal to the goblin combat value. Example: 0.80.
Elite power (combat)
Real combat power of elite orcs in auto-resolve. Drives how much they actually win/lose simulated battles. Example: 2.80 means one elite counts as ~2.8 average troops.
Orc power (combat)
Real combat power of regular orcs in auto-resolve. Example: 1.60 = one orc counts as ~1.6 average troops.
Goblin power (combat)
Real combat power of goblins in auto-resolve. Example: 0.80 = one goblin counts as ~0.8 of an average troop.
Enemy orc power vs you (auto-resolve)

Combat power of orcs fighting YOU in auto-resolve. The combat buff also applies when you auto-resolve, but the live-battle nerf for enemy orcs does NOT, so a small orc army could beat your large one on auto. Default 1.4 = orcs a bit scarier than vanilla, but numbers still win (190 orcs x1.4 = 266 < your 540). 1.0 = pure vanilla. Raise to make enemy orcs scarier on auto-resolve. Does not affect AI-vs-AI battles.
AI castle hire chance
Hourly chance for an AI orc lord standing in a castle to buy one elite orc. Higher = AI fields elites faster. Example: 0.25 = 25% per hour in a castle.
Virtual hire chance
Hourly 'as if visiting' hire chance when the lord is NOT physically in a castle but the clan owns one. Keeps AI elites flowing while lords roam. Example: 0.05 = 5% per hour.
AI gold buffer (castle)
Minimum gold an AI keeps after buying an elite in a castle, so it never goes broke. Example: 500 = the AI only buys if it stays above 500 gold.
AI goblin hire chance
Hourly chance for an AI orc lord in a village to buy a pair of goblins. Example: 0.10 = 10% per hour.
Goblin pair price
Gold an AI pays per pair of goblins in a village. Lower = AI fields more goblins. Example: 50 gold per pair.
Goblins per hire
How many goblins the AI gets per village purchase. Example: 2 goblins each time it buys.
AI gold buffer (village)
Minimum gold an AI keeps after buying goblins in a village. Example: 200 = it only buys above 200 gold.
AI troll recruit chance
Chance per visit that an AI orc lord picks up a troll in an orc settlement. Low on purpose, trolls are rare and strong. Example: 0.02 = 2% per visit.
AI troll cap per party
Maximum trolls a single AI orc lord can hold. Example: 1 = at most one troll per AI party, so armies are not troll-stacked.
Upgrade cost multiplier
Multiplier on the vanilla upgrade-cost formula for orc troops. Higher = upgrading orcs costs more gold. Example: 1.0 = vanilla cost.
Orc upgrade min cost
Minimum gold an orc upgrade can cost, so cheap tiers are never almost free. Example: 50 = no orc upgrade costs less than 50 gold.
Goblin upgrade min cost
Minimum gold a goblin upgrade can cost. Example: 50 gold floor per goblin upgrade.
AI elite discount
Fraction of the elite-orc price the AI pays (1.0 = full price). Lower = AI fields elites cheaper than you. Example: 0.60 = AI pays 60% of the elite cost.
Meat per regular prisoner
Meat gained from butchering a regular prisoner (cannibalism economy). Example: 1 meat per normal prisoner chopped.
Meat per large prisoner
Meat gained from butchering a large prisoner such as a troll or minotaur. Example: 3 meat per large prisoner.
Brawl gold multiplier
Multiplier on the gold reward for winning a tavern brawl. Lower curbs easy gold farming. Example: 0.50 = half the vanilla brawl payout.
Shrine loot gold
Gold reward for looting a shrine. Example: 400 gold per shrine looted.
Min tier for Extorsion
Only orcs of this tier or higher generate teef via Extorsion. Higher = fewer low-tier orcs earn teef. Example: 4 = only Tier 4+ orcs extort.
Hideout respawn delay (days)
Days a cleared bandit hideout stays gone before it can reappear. Higher = cleared hideouts stay gone longer. Example: 60 days (~2 months).
Max bandit hideouts (per faction)

How many hideouts ONE bandit faction (deserters, cultists, orc raiders, etc.) can keep active on the map at once. TOR raises the vanilla 9 to 30, which floods the map — this caps it back. Lower = fewer hideouts. Extra hideouts you already have thin out as you clear them. Example: 7.
Bandit patrols per hideout
How many roaming bandit parties can spawn around EACH active hideout. TOR default is 6 — lower to thin out the parties wandering the map. Example: 3.
Max looter/deserter parties
Hard cap on looter and deserter parties on the map. TOR allows up to 200 — this only lowers it (never raises it above what TOR's campaign stage permits). Example: 60.
Extra companion slots

Extra companion (wanderer) slots for YOUR clan on top of the vanilla limit. 0 = vanilla. Example: 10 lets you keep 10 more companions than your clan tier normally allows. Applies to the player clan only.
Max troll raid parties
How many raid parties can be alive from a single troll cave at once. Example: 2 raiding parties per cave.
Daily spawn chance
Daily chance for a troll cave to spawn a new raid party. Example: 0.05 = 5% per day.
Cave defender size
How many trolls greet you when you enter a troll cave. Higher = a bigger defending force. Example: 50.
Max trolls per raid party
Cap on the size of a single troll raid party. Example: 10 trolls maximum per raiding party.
Troops into cave battle
How many of your troops you can bring into the cave clear-out fight (vanilla 20). 7 trolls can crush 20 men even one-on-one, so more gives a numeric chance. Example: 40.
Troll luring allowed
If ON, adds a 'Lure trolls (10 meat)' option to the cave menu. Off by default because a Tier-6 troll for 10 meat is exploitable.
Orc HP buff
Structural toggle: turn the entire orc HP-buff system on or off. OFF = orcs use vanilla HP regardless of the HP sliders.
Team-aware damage
Structural toggle for all orc damage modifiers (ally/enemy attacker and victim multipliers). OFF = vanilla orc damage.
Structural toggle for the orc teef currency economy (earning and spending teef). OFF = orcs use plain gold like vanilla.
Orc army caps
Structural toggle for orc party-size and army caps. OFF = AI orc parties grow without our limits.
Troll cave nerf
Structural toggle for the troll-cave rebalance (battle size, raid limits). OFF = vanilla troll-cave behavior.
Hideout cooldown
Structural toggle for the hideout cooldown/limit system. OFF = vanilla hideout respawn rules.
Brawl reward nerf
Structural toggle for the reduced brawl gold reward. OFF = vanilla brawl payout.
Shrine loot nerf
Structural toggle for the shrine-loot rebalance (gold only, no meat/teef). OFF = vanilla shrine loot.
Career quest nerf
Lowers the requirements of orc career quests (weapon-skill training counts, battles, duels, teef earned). OFF = vanilla quest requirements.
Orcs without trebuchets
Orcs do not build complex siege engines, only ballistas and battering rams. OFF = orcs can build trebuchets as in vanilla.
Axe breaks shields
Structural toggle for the axe-versus-wooden-shield bonus damage. OFF = axes deal vanilla shield damage.
Mace/hammer breaks metal shields
Toggle for the blunt-weapon-versus-metal-shield bonus damage (maces, hammers). OFF = blunt weapons deal vanilla shield damage.
Lore-Hardcore: compatibility check
Could not begin the duel here. Challenge the Overlord out in the open field.
Some Lore-Hardcore features are being blocked by another mod:
The map time panel (extra speed button and Chaos progress bar) could not be added.
Possible cause: {LIST}
Load Lore-Hardcore AFTER TOR. Libraries like UIExtenderEx, MCM and ButterLib are required and fine - do not disable them. If a feature stays broken, try disabling UI/combat overhaul mods.
Faster goblins
Structural toggle for the goblin speed/athletics buffs. OFF = goblins move at vanilla speed.
Long kick stun
Structural toggle for the longer orc kick and shield-bash stun. OFF = vanilla stun durations.
Chaos recruiting
Legacy toggle, now covered by the Chaos master switch on the Main tab. Left in place for save compatibility.
Random brawl spawn
If ON, you spawn at a random point in the brawl arena instead of a fixed spot. Cosmetic variety only.
Teef icon
If ON, the hire menu shows a teef icon instead of the gold coin for orc recruitment. Cosmetic.
Enable orc overhaul
Master switch for ALL orc-race changes. OFF turns off every orc patch and behavior, so greenskins use pure vanilla TOR mechanics. Requires a game restart.
Enable chaos overhaul

Master switch for ALL Chaos-race changes. OFF turns off every chaos patch and behavior. Requires a game restart. Covers hiring Chaos units, peace with dark factions, friendly cultist hideouts, the Gift of Chaos, and more.
Tribute for vassalage (gold)
Gold a Chaos Lord demands as a gift to the Gods before accepting you as a vassal. Higher = a longer grind before you can join Chaos. Example: 50000 gold.
Min clan tier for vassalage
Minimum clan tier you need to request vassalage from a Chaos Lord. Example: 2 = your clan must be at least tier 2.
Min clan tier for Gift of Chaos
Minimum clan tier you need to undergo the conversion ritual into the chaos culture. Example: 3.
Skill XP boost on conversion
XP each of your combat skills gains the moment you accept the Gift of Chaos. Higher = a bigger one-time power spike. Example: 5000 XP per skill.
Tempting progress per day
Tempting progress added per day while a lord is your captive. The bar fills toward conversion. Example: 1.5 per day, about 67 days to the Gift.
Bonus for chaos player
Extra tempting progress if you are already of the chaos culture (after the Gift). Example: 0.5 = +50% faster conversions.
Conversion threshold
Progress a captive must reach to be ready to accept the Gift of Chaos. Higher = longer captivity needed. Example: 100.
Penalty per previous conversion
Each lord you have already converted slows further conversions by this fraction, so each one is harder. Example: 0.10 = -10% rate per prior conversion.
Progress decay per day when free
If a captive is freed or escapes, their tempting progress drops by this much per day. Example: 5 per day.
Norscan HP buff
HP buff for all Norscan units (tribesman to champion). Example: 0.30 = +30% HP.
Beastmen Gor HP buff
HP buff for Beastmen Gor (gor, foerender, manripper). Example: 0.25 = +25% HP.
Minotaur HP buff
HP buff for Minotaurs, lore-massive beasts. Example: 0.50 = +50% HP.
Units per +1 party cap
How many Cultists or Ungors are needed for +1 to your party limit (the fodder mechanic). Lower = cheaper cap growth. Example: 2 fodder per +1.
Minotaur recruit chance (castle)
Chance a Minotaur is offered in slot 5 of a castle town's recruits when you visit. Example: 0.20 = 20% chance on visit.
Cooldown between Chaos uprisings (days)
Days between Chaos Uprisings. Vanilla TOR is 126 days (~4 months); our default 30 (~1 month) makes Chaos joinable early. Applied on a new campaign.
Greenskin appetite
The Dark Gods savour the sacrifice. +{DE} Dark Energy.
The temptation is complete
{HERO} is broken in captivity and ready to accept the Gift of Chaos. Convert them and take them into your ranks?
Not yet
-{DE} Dark Energy for {COUNT}x {NAME}.
Ungor Barbed Arrows
Norscan Hunter
Norscan Berserker
Norscan Savage
Norscan Warrior
Norscan Thrower
Norscan Champion Thrower
Norscan Rider
Maybe later, gobbo.
Eh boss, I already gived ya teefz yesterdai! Come back in {DAYS} dayz, I needz ta dig more, see?
Ain't got dat much shinies yet.
Oi BIG boss! Gotz teef fer ya shinies, wanna trade, eh? Hurr hurr!
Hurr hurr! No shinies, no teefz. Go pinch some humies first, ya hear?
Trade 1000 shinies fer {AMOUNT} teefz.
*The goblin counts out {NOMINAL} teef, slips {HIDDEN} coins into his sneaky pouch on the side.* Pleazure doin' bizniss wif ya, BIG boss!
Walk away
Goblin Changer
*The goblin counts out {NOMINAL} teef on the tavern table, then quickly slips {HIDDEN} coins into a pouch on his belt before pushing the rest over to you.* Pleazure doin' bizniss wif ya, BIG boss!

*A cunning goblin at the corner table waves you over, fingering a heap of small coins. He whispers:* Oi BIG boss! Gotz teef fer ya shinies. 1000 shinies — and I count out {NOMINAL}. Will I slip {HIDDEN} into me own pouch on da side? Maybe... Wanna trade or wot?
Maybe later
Talk to da goblin changer in da tavern
*A goblin in the corner sniffs at your purse and snorts.* Hurr hurr! No shinies, no teefz. Go pinch some humies first, ya hear?
*A goblin in the corner of the tavern eyes you suspiciously and turns away.* Eh boss, I already gived ya teefz couple days back! Come in {DAYS} more days, see?
Greetinz, BIG boss! Da boyz watch me deal here — straight 'n fair, no funny bizniss. Wanna change ya shinies fer teef?
*Goblin counts out exactly {AMOUNT} teef into ya palm — straight 'n honest, da brutes are watchin'.* Anyfing else, BIG boss?
Trade 1000 shinies fer {AMOUNT} teef.
Oi BIG boss! Gotz teef fer ya shinies, wanna trade, eh? Hurr hurr!
So, how about a trade?
Sorry boss, I dun got no more teef left fer today. Come back tomorrow when I dig up more from da boyz, eh?
The Chaos allies are pleased with your aid in battle.
The Gods whisper your name. Accept the Gift of Chaos, and I will free you as a brother.
...Yes. I hear them. I can resist no longer. Take my oath — I am yours.
The lord accepts the Gift of Chaos and joins your side.
Make me one of you.
The ritual is irreversible. Your mortal nature will die. Are you ready?
I am ready.
I need to think about it.
It is done. You are part of the True Path.
The Gift of Chaos accepted
Talk (we both stand under the gaze of the Gods)
What brings you to our lands, mortal? Speak quickly — the Gods dislike idle words.
I wish to serve the Dark Gods.
We hold no lands yet, mortal — there is nowhere to take you in. Return when the Gods grant us a stronghold.
Serve my throne as a mercenary.
The Gods favor your gold. Our blades are yours, my lord.
Where is your overlord right now?
My overlord is {LEADER_LOC}. I am merely his hand.
Just passing by.
Pass peacefully (the Dark Gods hear your blood)
Summon the servants of Chaos from the lair
Bow before the portal and pass peacefully
Summon the Chosen of Chaos from the portal
This place has not yet gathered its strength. Return later.
Not enough gold for the offering.
{NAME} emerges from the portal.
Summoned: {LIST}.
I wish to serve the Dark Gods.
The throne of Chaos belongs to the strongest. I challenge you to battle!
Insolent mortal! So be it — the Gods thirst for blood. Meet me outside the walls, on the open ground. Come when you are ready — one of us will not see the sunset.
This throne will be mine.
Not yet.
I challenge you again!
You have already tempted your fate and were struck down, miserable worm! The Dark Gods see your deceit — here is your reckoning.
The Dark Gods have turned from the coward: your Chaos Glory dims.
The Gods have punished your insolence. Heal your wounds and prove you are worthy of the throne.
You are struck down by the Chaos Overlord. Return in {DAYS} days to challenge him again.
You have torn the throne from the dead hands of the Chaos Overlord! The Dark Gods acknowledge a new lord. The kingdom is yours.
The Usurper Ascends the Throne
The Dark Gods gaze upon you! Find the Chaos Overlord — the ruler of a Chaos kingdom — speak with him and challenge him for the throne.
Chaos Glory
Challenge Ready
In Disgrace
Cursed Markets

Chaos arms and armor are shunned by honest folk and appear for sale ONLY in the markets of Chaos-held towns. Marauder gear, cultist robes, chaos weapons, shields and a chaos steed are stocked in any Chaos town; the blessed war-plate of the Dark Gods (Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch, Slaanesh) and the helms of the Chosen only in the capital — the most prosperous Chaos town — and for a fortune.\nAnyone may buy in a Chaos town, but cursed goods cannot be SOLD anywhere except Chaos towns and villages — no other merchant will touch them.
Overthrow the Overlord

Might makes right. Slay enough foes with your own hand and the Dark Gods will mark you as a rival to the reigning Chaos Overlord.\nYour personal kills build a Chaos Glory, shown on the bar above the map clock. Serve Chaos at least 30 days and raise your Chaos Glory above the Overlord's. When the Gods take notice, challenge the Overlord to mortal combat: you against the Overlord and two of his Chaos Warriors.\nWin and his throne, fiefs and vassals are yours, and you take his blessed war-gear as a trophy. Lose and your Chaos Glory is broken by a third.
Hah! Bring me {VASSAL_GOLD} gold, and I will accept your oath.
Here is the gold.
I do not have that much.
I will think on it.
Excellent! The Gods accept your oath.
Return when you are ready.

The Chaos lords arm their new servant with a marauder's kit — a northern axe, shield and furs now lie in your inventory. Equip them (press I). The blessed war-gear of the Dark Gods is earned in blood, not handed to the untested.

Dark Energy is the mystic power of forbidden cults. Necromancers use it to raise the dead and sustain the undead. Servants of the Dark Gods draw upon it to enchant weapons and train chosen warriors. Sources: spilled blood, defiled places of power, dark rituals.
What do you want, mortal?
Tell me about yourself, traveler.
Let's get to business. I have work for you.
I need your skills. Will you come with me?
I have walked a long road in the name of the Dark Gods.
Blood and glory — that is all that matters now.
The weak serve, the strong rule. Which are you?
Enough talk. I will fight for you — for the right price.
I respect strength. Tell me more.
Spare me the tales. To business.
Another time, then.
My blade is yours — for {GOLD_AMOUNT}.
Done. {GOLD_AMOUNT} is yours.
I cannot pay that just now.
Chaos cannon fodder
Minotaur weight (eased)
Lore-Hardcore Guide
Orc Overhaul — Overview

Lore-Hardcore reworks the greenskins from a horde of cannon fodder into a proper Warhammer faction.

The core ideas:
- Orcs are tough and hit hard, but they are EXPENSIVE to field and slow to grow, so you never drown the map in free orcs.
- Orcs do not respect gold. In orc lands you pay in 'teef' (teeth), a currency you earn by fighting (see the Tooth Economy section).
- Elite orcs (Black Orcs, Bosses, Big'Uns) are fearless and brutal, but their numbers are capped so the AI cannot snowball.
- Many small flavour systems: cannibalism on the march, the brawl arena, castle duels, a reworked siege ram, and an orc career path.

Each topic on the left explains one system: what it does, why it exists, and how to use it. If you have never played greenskins before, read 'Tooth Economy' and 'Career' first.
Tooth Economy (Teef)

For orc and goblin heroes the Tooth Economy is ALWAYS on. There is no setup dialog and no mid-game switch. Humans, dwarves and vampires keep using gold as normal.

When you play an orc:
- In orc settlements you pay TEEF instead of gold for almost everything (recruiting elite orcs, duels, the brawl).
- Outside orc lands you still pay gold for ordinary services.
- Goblins are still hired for gold (they are cheap rabble).

How you EARN teef:
- Knocking out tough enemies in battle (Tier 4 and up).
- Killing enemy lords.
- Winning brawls and castle duels.
- A daily tribute from every orc town or castle you own.

Why it exists: in Warhammer, orcs literally use teeth as money — they do not value gold. The economy also keeps the AI in check: orc lords drain no gold from your clan (their wage-morale penalty is suppressed because you never actually pay them), and daily teef income is capped so AI warbosses cannot buy an endless elite army.
Career: Bully and Shaman

An orc hero picks one of two career paths at character creation.

Path of the Bully (becomes a Boss): a melee bruiser who grows by fighting — weapon skills, battles, tournaments, brawls and teef earned.

Path of the Shaman: a spellcaster who grows through faith and magic — Spellcraft, Faith, enchantments, teef, and shrines looted.

Lore-Hardcore LOWERS the vanilla TOR career requirements so the orc career is actually reachable in a normal playthrough. Roughly:
- Boss Tier 2: weapon skills ~80/80/30, 30 battles, 5 tournaments, 10 brawls, 2000 teef.
- Boss Tier 3: ~150/120/60, 70 battles, 15 tournaments, 20 brawls, 5 lord duels, 5 cities taken, 10000 teef.
- Shaman Tier 2: pray at a shrine, Spellcraft 50, Faith 50, 3 enchantments, 2000 teef, 5 shrines looted.
- Shaman Tier 3: Spellcraft 120, Faith 100, 8 enchantments, 10000 teef, 5 cities, 30 shrines.

The brawl arena and castle duels exist partly to help you tick off these objectives.
Morale and WAAAGH!

Orc combat and AI behaviour are rebalanced so greenskins feel dangerous without conquering the whole map.

On the battlefield:
- Orc lords get roughly +40% HP and +30% to combat skills as a baseline.
- Elite orcs (Black Orc, Boss, Berzerka, Big'Un) are FEARLESS and never panic.
- A WAAAGH! morale bonus builds as massed orcs pile into a fight.

So the AI does not snowball:
- A soft cap on AI orc lord party size (around 80 troops by default; you can change it in settings).
- A marshal's army holds only a handful of parties, so orcs attack in war-bands of 400-500 rather than 3000.
- The AI strength estimate counts orcs, goblins and big monsters at their true weight, so imperial lords correctly judge an orc threat and do not throw themselves away.

Your own party uses the normal vanilla size limits — these caps are for the AI.
Castle Duel Challenge

Visit an orc castle and you can challenge one of its elite orcs to a one-on-one duel for a teef wager.

- Win: the defeated elite joins your party as a trophy unit, and you gain teef.
- Lose: your wager is forfeit and your standing in the castle drops for a while.

The fight is a real 1v1 on a castle scene — bring your A-game. Which elites are on offer depends on the castle (Big'Un, Boss, Black Orc, Brute, Berzerka), and tougher foes cost a bigger wager. It is a fast way to earn an elite unit and to complete the duel objective of the orc career.
Sieges and the Ram

Orcs besiege differently from civilised factions.

- Orcs do NOT build trebuchets — they have no patience for delicate engineering. They use ballistas, onagers and catapults (including fire variants).
- Their battering ram is massively buffed: it hits far harder and survives far longer, so gates fall in a handful of hits instead of dozens.
- Auto-resolve gets the same ram buff, so an orc siege resolves quickly whether you watch it or not.

The idea is a brutal, fast, smash-the-gate orc assault rather than a slow artillery duel.
Prisoner Cannibalism

Orcs do NOT butcher prisoners in the loot screen. Meat comes ONLY from hunger on the march.

After a battle you get zero meat from chopping prisoners or from the auto-loot — that path is deliberately closed for the player.

The only way to get meat from captives: take prisoners, run out of food, and keep marching. Each hour with an empty larder your party eats one mouth:
1. Prisoners first (each gives 1 meat).
2. Then goblins (each gives 1 meat).
3. Lords and companions are NEVER eaten — a lord is worth a ransom, a companion is part of your story.

Lore: orcs devour captives alive on the road, not neatly at a butcher's block. AI orc parties keep the vanilla meat-from-battle so they do not starve to death across the campaign.
Greenskin Brawl Arena

The brawl is a fist-fight arena found in orc settlements.

- Around 18 enemies per match.
- Your reward scales with how many you personally knock out.
- Enemies who run out of bounds are auto-finished so a match cannot stall.

It is a great place to train One-Handed, earn teef, and tick off the brawl objective of the orc career. In conquered non-orc cities the brawl's atmosphere effects are suppressed to avoid graphics errors on older machines.
What is Chaos

Chaos is the dark-power faction overhaul: the warriors, beasts and cultists who serve the four Dark Gods of the north. It bundles together Norscan tribesmen, Beastmen (Gor, Ungor, Minotaurs), Chaos Warriors and the hidden cults that worship in secret across the civilised lands.

Chaos is a BETA feature and is OFF by default. Turn it on with the 'Enable chaos overhaul' master switch in the settings (it needs a game restart). With it off, greenskins and everyone else behave normally and none of the systems below apply.

Two ways to engage with Chaos:
1. Fight it — Chaos is at war with the whole world. Stand against the invasions and the cult uprisings.
2. Join it — become a servant of the Dark Gods: take the Gift of Chaos, serve a Chaos lord, recruit dark troops, and turn captured lords to your side.

This guide explains, step by step, how to become a Chaos player and every system that comes with it. Start with 'Getting Started / Joining' and read down the list.
Getting Started / Joining

You do not start as Chaos — you choose to fall.

There are two routes into the fold:

1) Become a vassal of a Chaos lord.
- While you are an INDEPENDENT clan (no kingdom of your own and not sworn to anyone), you are not forced into permanent war with Chaos, so you can actually walk up and talk to a Chaos lord.
- Find a lord whose culture is Chaos, open dialogue, and choose 'I wish to serve the Dark Gods'.
- He demands a tribute of gold as an offering to the Gods. Pay it and you join his kingdom as a vassal. The amount and the minimum clan tier are set in the settings.

2) Take the Gift of Chaos directly (see the next section) to convert your own hero and clan to the chaos culture.

Important: once you belong to a Chaos kingdom, the whole world is at war with you — that is intended (see 'War and Diplomacy'). Make sure you are ready for that fight before you swear the oath.
The Gift of Chaos

The Gift of Chaos is a ritual that turns your hero — and your whole clan — into the chaos culture. It is the deep commitment to the Dark Gods.

What it does:
- Changes your hero's culture to chaos, and crucially your CLAN's culture too. This matters: if only the hero changed, fiefs you hold would suffer a loyalty mismatch. Both are converted so chaos settlements stay loyal to you.
- Tries to change your appearance toward a chaos-touched look where the game supports it.
- Grants a burst of combat experience, reflecting the raw power the Gods pour into the newly faithful.

How to get it: ask a Chaos lord to 'make you one of them' in dialogue (a minimum clan tier applies, set in settings). The conversion is permanent.

After the Gift you can recruit dark troops freely in chaos lands, pass cultist lairs peacefully, and you start generating Dark Energy.
Dark Energy

Dark Energy is the resource of the Chaos player — the favour of the Dark Gods made manifest. It works like the orcs' teef or the vampires' winds: a special currency only your faction uses.

How you EARN it:
- Sacrificing prisoners by execution (the Gods savour the offering).
- Other dark deeds as the faction grows.

What you SPEND it on:
- Upgrading elite Chaos troops. Promoting a unit into a true Chaos elite costs Dark Energy on top of the normal upgrade. If you cannot afford it, the upgrade is blocked in the UI until you have enough — so you cannot go into debt.

Dark Energy never drops below zero, and you only see it once you are aligned with Chaos. Think of it as the price the Gods charge for their finest champions: you must keep feeding them to keep growing.
Chaos Troops

The armies of Chaos mix cheap fodder with terrifying elites.

The roster, roughly from weakest to strongest:
- Cultists and Acolytes — human fanatics, pure cannon fodder.
- Ungors — the smaller beastmen, also fodder.
- Norscan tribesmen and their champions — the human warriors of the north. They have been given a HP buff so a Norscan is sturdier than an ordinary man, as the lore demands.
- Gor and their elites — the larger beastmen, with a HP buff above the Norscans.
- Chaos Warriors — armoured champions of the Dark Gods.
- Minotaurs — massive monsters with the biggest HP buff of all.

The fodder mechanic: Cultists and Ungors take LESS room in your party. For every couple of fodder units you carry, your party limit rises by one — exactly like the small undead of the vampire faction. This keeps the cheap rabble playable instead of wasting precious party slots.

All the HP buffs are adjustable in the settings if you want a different balance.
Recruiting Chaos Troops

Once you are aligned with Chaos (a vassal/overlord, or of the chaos culture) you gain several ways to raise dark armies. You cannot do any of this as an outsider — the cults only open up to the faithful.

Ways to recruit:
- Settlements: notables in chaos-aligned towns, castles and villages offer dark volunteers instead of normal recruits. Castle areas lean toward elite warriors; towns mix in fodder.
- Cultist and beastmen LAIRS (the bandit-style hideouts of forest and steppe cults): instead of fighting them, you can pass peacefully and even summon their servants for gold. There is a cooldown so you cannot farm one lair forever.
- Chaos PORTALS: special sites where you can bow and pass, or summon the Chosen of Chaos — the elite of the elite — for a price.
- Minotaurs appear as a rare recruit slot when you visit a chaos castle.

Which fodder a place offers depends on its culture (steppe lairs give Ungors, forest cults give Cultists).
Tempting Captives

As a Chaos player you can corrupt the lords you capture, turning enemy nobles into servants of the Dark Gods instead of just ransoming them.

How it works:
- Hold an enemy lord prisoner. Each day in your dungeon adds 'tempting' progress as the Gods whisper to them.
- When progress crosses the threshold, the captive is ready to accept the Gift of Chaos. You get the option to convert them — they break and join your side.
- If the captive is freed or escapes, their progress decays.
- Each lord you have already converted makes the next one slightly harder, so you cannot endlessly mass-convert the whole world.

All the numbers — progress per day, the threshold, the per-conversion penalty, the decay rate, and a bonus if you are already of the chaos culture — are tunable in the settings. By default it takes a couple of months of captivity to turn a lord.
Vassalage and Rewards

When you swear yourself to a Chaos kingdom you are not sent away empty-handed — the Dark Gods arm their new champion.

On joining a Chaos kingdom your hero is granted a starter set of Chaos gear: armour, helms and weapons themed after the four gods (Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch, Slaanesh) plus universal Chaos Warrior pieces and a mount. Equip whichever set you like.

Serving Chaos also shifts your relationships — the dark lords welcome you as kin, while the civilised world turns against you.

This reward is granted once, the first time your clan joins a chaos-culture kingdom. If the items ever fail to appear, the mod logs a warning at startup so the problem is visible (it checks every gear id when the game loads).
Chaos Uprisings

Chaos does not sit still. Periodically a Chaos Uprising erupts — a wave of dark forces that bursts onto the map to threaten the civilised realms.

In vanilla TOR the first uprising is far off (around 126 days). Lore-Hardcore shortens the gap (default about 30 days, set in settings) so Chaos is a living threat much sooner and the world feels under genuine pressure.

The cooldown applies from the start of a new campaign: the first uprising happens after your chosen number of days, and they recur on that timer. Lower it for a frantic, Chaos-haunted world; raise it for a slower burn.
War and Diplomacy

The most important thing to understand before you join: the ENTIRE world is at war with Chaos, and once you belong to a Chaos kingdom, that means the entire world is at war with YOU.

This is intended. Chaos is the great enemy of every civilised faction — there is no lasting peace to be had as a sworn servant of the Dark Gods. Expect to be attacked from all sides.

The one exception is BEFORE you join: while you are still an independent clan with no kingdom, the mod does not force you into war with Chaos. That window is what lets you ride up to a Chaos lord and negotiate joining in the first place. The moment you swear the oath, the gloves come off and the world unites against you.

Plan accordingly: build a strong, self-sufficient dark army (fodder for numbers, elites and monsters for punch) before you take the plunge, because you will be fighting everyone.
I have no room for you — my retinue is already full.
Then return when you have made room, mortal. The Dark Gods do not like to wait.
Where might I find your master, the Overlord of Chaos?
Our lord holds court in {SETTLEMENT}.
Our lord is on the march, near {SETTLEMENT}.
Our lord lies captive in the dungeons of {SETTLEMENT}.
Our lord roams the wilds — even I know not where.
{DAYS}d of service left
The Throne Awaits
You have proven yourself in blood and service. Seek out the Chaos Overlord — ask any Chaos lord where he holds court — and challenge him for his throne.
I would face you again, Chaos Overlord.
You already tasted defeat, mortal. Lick your wounds and return when you are worthy — in {DAYS} days. Begone!
I leave... in shame.
challenge in {DAYS}d
Draw your weapon — begin the duel.
Choose your champions
Pick who fights at your side (up to {MAX}). The Chaos Overlord brings an equal guard. Choose none to face him alone.
To battle!
The cultists of Chaos have raised a ring of skulls and black iron upon the field. The Chaos Overlord {KING} steps in to face you — only one of you will walk out of the ring alive.
The Chaos Overlord lies dead at your feet. The Dark Gods roar their approval — the path to your own throne is open.
Claim the throne of Chaos.
You are beaten and cast down. Lick your wounds and return in {DAYS} days to try again.
Found Your Kingdom of Chaos
The throne is yours by blood. Name your new kingdom of Chaos.
Host of Chaos
Your kingdom of Chaos is founded. The Dark Gods watch their new champion.
Not today — I withdraw. (leave)
Tier too low — only T4+ orcs have golden teef worth taking.
Dark Gods
Kills for your God's war-set

When you pledge to Chaos you choose one of four Dark Gods (Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch, Slaanesh). Once your personal kill count reaches this number, your god rewards you with their full top-tier set of armour and weapon. Lower = you earn the elite gear sooner. Example: 1000 means about 1000 enemies slain by your own hand. Range 500-2000.
Extra kills to challenge the Overlord

Additional personal kills, ON TOP of the war-set threshold, before you may challenge the Chaos Overlord (Mortkin) to a duel for his throne. Total kills for the duel = war-set threshold + this value. Lower = the throne challenge unlocks sooner. Example: with both at 1000, you challenge him at 2000 kills. Range 500-2000.
Khorne, the Blood God
Nurgle, the Plague Lord
Tzeentch, the Changer of Ways
Slaanesh, the Dark Prince
No god
Skulls for the skull throne. Khorne favours the relentless butcher who takes heads in open slaughter.
The Grandfather of plagues embraces the enduring. His chosen rot, yet refuse to fall.
The Architect of Fate rewards the cunning sorcerer who bends the winds of magic to his will.
The Prince of Pleasure prizes the swift and the perfect - the duelist who makes killing an art.
Pledge to a Dark God

You kneel before the altar of Chaos. Choose the god whose mark you will bear — this choice is final. At {SET} kills your chosen god grants you their full war-panoply; at {DUEL} kills you may challenge the Overlord himself for his throne.
Swear the oath
You are now sworn to {GOD}. {FLAVOR}
The Dark Gods reward your slaughter - the full war-panoply of {GOD} now lies in your inventory. Equip it from your inventory (press I).
A God's Favour
